steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by making it a command - notice the 'u' sound at start and the quiet middle
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' and changed vowels to show present tense. This word is what I say when following the command قُلْ (say!).

steps.m: lord (one who nurtures and cares for others)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who intensely/completely has this quality of nurturing and caring for others
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'

steps.m: by the Lord (object of أَعُوذُ - I seek refuge)
steps.n: The بِ at the start means 'by' or 'with'. This word is connected to أَعُوذُ (I seek refuge) in the ayah.

steps.m: to split open, to cleave, to cause daybreak

steps.n: Adding the basic vowel marks turns it into a complete past tense verb

steps.m: the daybreak (object of بِرَبِّ - Lord of)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific 'the daybreak', and ِ to show it follows 'Lord of' (بِرَبِّ). This word is the object being possessed in the phrase 'Lord of the daybreak'.
This is a basic preposition that shows the starting point or source of something. Here it connects to شَرِّ (evil) to show what we're seeking protection from.
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes the meaning stronger
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show intensity

steps.m: of intense evil/harm (connected to مِن before it)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah
This is a connecting word that links 'evil of' with 'He created', showing we're talking about the evil of things that were created
In this ayah, مَا is being used to refer to all created things in general, not asking a question

steps.m: he created (what is referred to in مَا)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a complete past-tense verb. This word refers to the creation of whatever is mentioned in مَا (what/that which) in this ayah.
This is the basic past tense form showing that someone (he) did the action of creating
This is a basic preposition that shows the starting point or source of something
The و (and) connects this to the previous statement about seeking refuge. This preposition introduces what we're seeking refuge from.
This is the basic concept of evil or harm
The last two letters combine with a shadda to intensify the meaning of evil

steps.m: of intense evil (connected to وَمِن meaning 'and from')
steps.n: The kasra shows this word is connected to the words before it (وَمِن) meaning 'and from'
This refers to darkness spreading or settling in
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show who/what does the action. This word describes something that causes or brings darkness.
Added tanween kasra because this word comes after شَرِّ (evil of) in the ayah, showing it's being possessed or owned by the previous word
This is the basic form of the word that points to a specific time
Adding ا at the end changes it from referring to a specific past time to indicating future events. In this ayah, it introduces when something will happen in the future - when darkness spreads.

steps.m: to enter or sink into darkness
steps.n: This describes something entering or penetrating deeply into something else

steps.m: it became dark (describes غَاسِقٍ - the night)
steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word describes how the night (غَاسِقٍ) becomes completely dark.
Basic preposition indicating source or origin. In this ayah, it connects to the word شَرِّ (evil/harm) that follows it, showing what we're seeking protection from
Added و at beginning to connect with previous statements about seeking protection. This links this source of evil with other things we seek protection from.
Basic three-letter root indicating evil or wickedness
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic noun
Doubled the middle letter ر to show intense or strong evil
Adding kasra at the end shows this word follows مِن (from/of) in the ayah

steps.m: to blow with puffs of breath
steps.n: Basic meaning of blowing or puffing with mouth
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added shadda and alif to show someone who does this action intensively or repeatedly
Added feminine plural ending to show this refers to females who do this action

steps.m: the female ones who blow repeatedly
steps.n: Added definite article to specify these particular blowers

steps.m: of the female ones who blow repeatedly (into knots)
steps.n: Added kasra at end because this word follows مِن شَرِّ (from the evil of). Added note about blowing into knots to show context of magic practice.
Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows the evil-doers are blowing into (في) the knots.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to tie or make a knot
steps.n: Basic meaning of tying or making knots
Connected the root letters together to make the basic noun for one knot
Changed the vowel pattern to make it plural, showing many knots instead of just one
Added ال to make it specific/definite knots
Added i-sound (kasra) at end because it comes after 'from' (min)
Basic preposition indicating source or origin. In this ayah, it connects to شَرِّ (evil/harm) that follows it.
Added و at beginning to connect it to previous statements about seeking refuge. This connects it to the earlier parts of the surah.
Basic three-letter root meaning evil or wickedness
Connected the letters and doubled the ر with shadda to show intense evil
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah

steps.m: to feel jealous of someone's blessings
steps.n: Basic three-letter root meaning envy
Connected the letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: someone who feels jealous (of others' blessings)
steps.n: Changed the word pattern to show who does the action. This describes the person doing the jealous feeling.

steps.m: a jealous person (connected to شَرِّ/evil of)
steps.n: Added the tanween kasra mark to show this word is owned by the word شَرِّ (evil) that comes before it in the ayah, making it mean 'evil of a jealous person'
Basic time word that points to when something happens
Adding ا makes it specifically about future or conditional time. Here it connects the 'envier' (حَاسِدٍ) with the action of 'envying' (حَسَدَ)

steps.m: to feel jealous of someone's blessings and wish they would lose them
steps.n: Basic three-letter root meaning envy

steps.m: he envied (action of حَاسِدٍ/envier mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This is the action being done by the حَاسِدٍ (envier) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
